items.title: IPC president disappointed with Ukraine, suggests team shifted focus to politics at Milan-Cortina Paralympics
items.summary_auto: The president of the International Paralympic Committee was disappointed with the complaints made by Ukraine's National Paralympic Committee during the Milan-Cortina Games, saying they appeared to be an attempt to shift the focus from sports to politics.

items.title: Tuition scams; Sobeys faces scrutiny from Manitoba; soaring plane ticket costs: CBC's Marketplace Cheat Sheet
items.summary_auto: The CBC's Marketplace rounds up the consumer and health news you need from the week.

items.title: Kashechewan chief walks back calls for Indigenous Services minister to resign
items.summary_auto: Kashechewan First Nation Chief Hosea Wesley says he takes back a call he made earlier in the month for Indigenous Services Canada Minister Mandy Gull-Masty to resign.

items.title: Backlot HFX bringing queer nightlife back to Gottingen Street
items.summary_auto: Michael Gouthro says he hopes there’ll be something for everyone at his new two-storey locale.

items.title: Withdrawn charges in Stronach sex assault trial raise questions about prosecution
items.summary_auto: When his trial began in February, Frank Stronach faced 12 counts related to seven female complainants whose allegations included sexual assault and the historical charges of rape and attempted rape. Now, with all the evidence heard and closing submissions set for the end of the month, those charges have been whittled down to seven — related to four of the initial seven complainants.

items.title: NASA's asteroid deflection test for planetary defence even more successful than we thought
items.summary_auto: The Double Asteroid Redirection Test (DART) mission not only altered an asteroid’s orbit but, for the first time, a new study shows we also successfully changed the asteroids' path around the sun.

items.title: Canada named this castle-like courthouse a national historic site. Now a tower is set to rise beside it
items.summary_auto: Plans for a 54-storey residential tower next door to a national historic site in London, Ont.,  cast a literal shadow over the place where the city began, while at the same time shining a light on what some heritage advocates say is a gap in federal law.

items.title: Inside the weird world of Toronto’s transit seat upholstery
items.summary_auto: Scoring a seat on a busy bus, streetcar or subway is a hot commodity, but have you ever thought about why they're made the way they are? Welcome to the weird world of transit seat upholstery.

items.title: I just wanted to be ‘one of the guys’ on my hockey team, but it wasn’t always easy
items.summary_auto: Trevor Kew has many fond recollections of playing minor hockey in Rossland, B.C., but these are tempered by less pleasant memories.

items.title: Mapping the global crackdown on shadow fleets
items.summary_auto: A CBC News analysis reveals the global scope of an enforcement crackdown on ships belonging to shadow fleets — vessels often flying under false flags in order to skirt sanctions — as the scale of seizures and detentions has skyrocketed in recent months.

items.title: The negative people in your life are making you get older faster. Here’s how to handle them
items.summary_auto:
Got a nagging father-in-law or a roommate who hounds you endlessly about how to properly load the dishwasher? Such people 
have something in common: they're hasslers, and they may be slowly shaving years off your life and putting you at a higher risk of developing chronic disease, according to a new study.

items.title: Has Trump pushed Canada and the U.K. into each other's arms?
items.summary_auto: Prime Minister Mark Carney is set to visit the U.K. on March 15 — his third trip since taking office — leaving some political observers wondering if this is a new era of a special relationship, replacing the one once held between the U.K. and the U.S.

items.title: Shadow fleets of sanctions-skirting ships facing global crackdown: analysis
items.summary_auto: A CBC News analysis reveals the global scope of an enforcement crackdown on ships belonging to shadow fleets — vessels often flying under false flags in order to skirt sanctions — as the scale of seizures and detentions has skyrocketed in recent months.

items.title: Alberta town wins $50K toward arena rebuild, holds street hockey fundraiser
items.summary_auto: A southern Alberta town that  lost the use of its aging hockey arena after an explosion has won thousands of dollars to use in its recovery efforts. Taber was revealed Saturday as one of the Kraft Hockeyville contest’s provincial winners  after a two-week judging process.

items.title: Emergency alert issued in Prince George, B.C. due to gas leak; residents told to evacuate
items.summary_auto: An emergency alert has been issued in Prince George due to a gas leak that officials say poses a threat to human life.

items.title: Hulu not moving forward with Buffy reboot, Sarah Michelle Gellar says
items.summary_auto: Hulu's reboot of Buffy the Vampire Slayer will no longer be happening, the TV show's former star announced on Saturday.

items.title: Canada's Steven Dubois wins 500m gold at world short track championships
items.summary_auto: Canada's Steven Dubois won gold in the 500 metres Saturday at the world short track speedskating championships. Dubois topped the podium in the same distance last month at the Milano-Cortina Olympic Games and successfully defended his world title in the event.
